pathway: P6d (FM-28 pathway family: P2b/P6d API authentication)

additional_traces.trace: Capture worker error category + http_status from tool response payload (or expose via REPL print)
additional_traces.rationale: PASS if category resolves to `AUTH` and status=401 for the injected fault; FAIL if category remains `UNKNOWN` or status is absent.
PASS if exactly 3 model calls occur (reasoning, worker fault, reasoning final) proving no hidden retry; FAIL if additional worker calls appear.
PASS if `_call_record.error_category` and `_call_record.http_status` preserve auth semantics; FAIL if callback receives non-int/opaque code and falls back to `UNKNOWN`.
PASS if bucket-level assertion catches category regressions; FAIL if only total-count assertion is used.

recommendations.action: Retain this fixture + dedicated FM-28 class as the worker-path auth error baseline (single dispatch, 401 fault, graceful completion).

resolution.evidence: Fixture exists (`tests_rlm_adk/fixtures/provider_fake/worker_auth_error_401.json:1`) and dedicated tests exist (`tests_rlm_adk/test_fmea_e2e.py:1973`); targeted run on 2026-03-03: `.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests_rlm_adk/test_fmea_e2e.py::TestWorkerAuthError401 -q` -> `6 passed in 2.61s`.
resolution.notes: PASS: FM-28 worker-path scenario is now executable and stable in CI-style targeted runs.

recommendations.action: Harden AUTH classification by normalizing `error.code` in worker callback classification (for example parse numeric status from provider exceptions before `code in (401, 403)` checks).

resolution.evidence: Current runtime for this fixture reports `category=UNKNOWN` and `error_counts {'UNKNOWN': 1}` despite injected 401 (plugin run output on 2026-03-03); classification logic currently depends on direct `getattr(error, "code", None)` checks (`rlm_adk/callbacks/worker.py:30`, `rlm_adk/callbacks/worker.py:35`).
resolution.notes: FAIL: AUTH branch is present in source but not reliably hit in fake-provider integration behavior.

recommendations.action: After classification hardening, tighten `TestWorkerAuthError401` to assert category bucket and propagated status (not just aggregate error count).

resolution.evidence: Current assertions only require non-empty `OBS_WORKER_ERROR_COUNTS` total (`tests_rlm_adk/test_fmea_e2e.py:2031`) and avoid strict AUTH expectation by design (`tests_rlm_adk/test_fmea_e2e.py:2020`).
resolution.notes: FAIL: Category/status regressions can pass unnoticed under the current relaxed assertions.

recommendations.action: Add direct worker classifier unit tests for 401/403 AUTH mapping and callback `_call_record.http_status` propagation.

resolution.evidence: Existing `_classify_error` unit coverage is parse/timeouts-oriented (`tests_rlm_adk/test_adk_callbacks.py:189`, `tests_rlm_adk/test_adk_callbacks.py:216`) and has no auth-branch assertions.
resolution.notes: FAIL: AUTH branch confidence currently depends on indirect integration behavior.

recommendations.action: Keep orchestrator-side FM-28 non-retry propagation test in regression suite and add 403 parity coverage.

resolution.evidence: 401 orchestrator path test exists (`tests_rlm_adk/test_bug001_orchestrator_retry.py:315`) and passes in targeted run on 2026-03-03: `.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests_rlm_adk/test_bug001_orchestrator_retry.py::TestNonTransient401Propagation::test_401_propagates_without_retry -q` -> `1 passed in 0.03s`.
resolution.notes: PASS for 401; 403 parity remains an incremental follow-up to fully match FM-28 401/403 scope.
